name: Logitech G PRO X SUPERLIGHT 2 Wireless Gaming Mouse

specs.Sensor: HERO 2 sensor — 32,000 DPI, 888 IPS, zero smoothing/filtering/acceleration
specs.Switches: LIGHTFORCE hybrid optical-mechanical switches — 2ms faster than standard mechanical

whatWorks: 60 grams is the gold standard for lightweight wireless mice, and the SUPERLIGHT 2 achieves it without sacrificing structural rigidity. The shell does not flex under grip pressure, the click buttons do not rattle, and the material quality matches premium peripherals that weigh 30-40 grams more. Logitech's engineering achievement here is making a 60g mouse that feels substantial rather than cheap.

The HERO 2 sensor with zero smoothing, filtering, or acceleration is the most accurate gaming sensor currently shipping in a consumer product. At any DPI setting from 100 to 32,000, the cursor tracks your physical hand movement with one-to-one precision — no prediction, no interpolation, no artificial smoothing that masks micro-tremors or introduces inconsistency. Professional players choose this sensor specifically because it gives them the truest representation of their own mechanics.

LIGHTFORCE hybrid optical-mechanical switches represent a meaningful advance in click technology. Standard mechanical switches have a debounce delay — a brief pause after the initial contact to prevent double-registering the vibration of the click mechanism. LIGHTFORCE uses an optical confirmation layer to detect intentional clicks faster, eliminating ~2ms of latency that mechanical switches inherently carry. In practice this means more responsive registration in games where click timing matters.

95-hour battery life with LIGHTSPEED wireless is exceptional — the original SUPERLIGHT's 70 hours was already class-leading, and the SUPERLIGHT 2 extends it to nearly four days of 8-hour daily gaming on a single charge. Combined with USB-C charging (and passtrough play-while-charging support), this mouse effectively never needs to be powered down for battery reasons.
worthKnowing: The SUPERLIGHT 2 has a subtle right-hand ergonomic bias in its shape despite being marketed as symmetrical. Left-handed users can use it comfortably, but the button layout is optimized for right-hand orientation. True ambidextrous options like the Razer DeathAdder V3 Hyperspeed or Logitech G305 are better choices for left-handed gamers.

No onboard RGB lighting — the SUPERLIGHT 2 is a purely performance-focused mouse with no light effects. If RGB aesthetics are important to your setup, this is explicitly not the right choice — Logitech's G502 X Plus or G403 provide RGB alongside competitive performance. The SUPERLIGHT 2 is designed around weight and performance, not visual appeal.

LIGHTSPEED wireless requires the USB dongle to maintain connection. The dongle is compact and can plug into a USB hub or the Logitech G POWERPLAY charging mat if your desktop has limited front USB access. Signal stability is excellent and does not degrade near WiFi routers or Bluetooth devices, but the dongle must remain within ~10 meters of the mouse.

The mouse is not water-resistant — it does not have an IP rating. Accidental spills should be avoided and the mouse should not be used in high-humidity environments. The PTFE feet are replaceable if worn, and Logitech sells replacement sets for long-term ownership.

commonComplaints.title: No RGB — purely utilitarian
commonComplaints.body: The SUPERLIGHT 2's lack of RGB is a deliberate design choice for weight savings, but it generates consistent complaints from buyers who expected the aesthetic options available on other premium gaming mice. The mouse is a tool, not a desk decoration.

commonComplaints.title: Side buttons could be more pronounced
commonComplaints.body: Some users find the side thumb buttons have less tactile feedback than competitors. The buttons are functional and reliable, but the click feel is shallower than Razer's side buttons, which some gamers notice coming from other mice.

commonComplaints.title: Price premium versus competitors
commonComplaints.body: At $189 CAD, the SUPERLIGHT 2 is priced at the top of the wireless gaming mouse market. Buyers comparing it to the Razer DeathAdder V3 HyperSpeed ($119) or Logitech G305 ($79) struggle to justify the premium for performance improvements that require very precise mechanical skill to perceive.

commonComplaints.title: No dongle storage in mouse
commonComplaints.body: Unlike some wireless mice that store the USB dongle inside the battery compartment, the SUPERLIGHT 2 has no dongle storage. The dongle is small enough to lose easily, and several buyers have replaced it after misplacing it during travel.

verdict: The Logitech G PRO X SUPERLIGHT 2 is the best wireless gaming mouse available, achieving the lightest competitive weight at 60g without compromising on sensor accuracy, switch performance, or battery life. LIGHTFORCE switches eliminate mechanical debounce delay and the HERO 2 sensor with zero processing is as accurate as gaming sensors get. It is expensive and deliberately utilitarian — no RGB, no extra buttons — but for competitive players who want the ceiling of mouse technology, nothing else comes this close.

realBuyers.quote: I have used every top mouse in the last three years. The SUPERLIGHT 2 is the first one I bought where I felt no desire to try something else. Sensor, switches, weight, battery — all best in class simultaneously.

The 60g weight makes extended gaming sessions dramatically less fatiguing. After 6 hours my hand and wrist feel noticeably better than with my 90g mouse. The ergonomic difference at lightweight is real.

LIGHTFORCE switches are genuinely faster than my old mechanical clicks. The response feels more immediate, which in Valorant means my click registration aligns more closely with my visual reaction time.
95 hours is not real — I cannot remember the last time I charged it. I charge once every two to three weeks of daily gaming. The LIGHTSPEED wireless is completely invisible in use.

metaDescription: Logitech G PRO X SUPERLIGHT 2 review: 60g wireless gaming mouse with HERO 2 sensor, LIGHTFORCE switches, and 95-hour battery. The best gaming mouse. $189 CAD on Amazon.ca.
introText: The Logitech G PRO X SUPERLIGHT 2 Wireless Gaming Mouse (ASIN B0BJ8PCDXJ) is available on Amazon.ca for $189 CAD and sets the bar for competitive wireless gaming mice with its 60g ultra-light shell, HERO 2 sensor with zero processing, LIGHTFORCE hybrid switches, and class-leading 95-hour battery life over LIGHTSPEED wireless. It is the mouse used by more professional esports players than any other.
